text: Elite marathon runners taper for approximately 14 days before competition, while middle and long distance runners taper for approximately 6 days.
During tapering, elite marathon runners reduce continuous running volume to approximately 53% of regular training levels, while middle and long distance runners reduce to approximately 70-71%.
Interval running volume is reduced during tapering to 53-67% of regular training levels across all endurance event distances.
During tapering, elite long distance and marathon runners maintain peak interval training intensity above race speed (112-114% of race pace), preserving or increasing intensity while reducing volume.
population: elite British endurance runners (long distance and marathon)

text: Runners with higher weekly continuous running volume and frequency during regular training make greater corresponding reductions during the taper (R = -0.70 and R = -0.63, respectively).
Running intensity during regular training is strongly predictive of taper running intensity, with correlations of R = 0.97 for continuous intensity and R = 0.81 for interval intensity.

The primary taper strategy for elite endurance runners involves reducing training volume while maintaining or slightly increasing training intensity, with greater volume reductions for longer race distances.
